title: sambucus mexicana var. typica
intro: Sambucus mexicana var. typica, also known as Mexican elderberry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Adoxaceae, native to Mexico and parts of Central America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiviral and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The plant's berries, leaves, and flowers have been used to make herbal preparations that help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and respiratory infections, as well as reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Mexican elderberry include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits and support overall well-being.
